entries.title: At his OpenAI trial, Musk relitigates an old friendship
entries.description: It's a story Musk has told before -- in interviews and to author Walter Isaacson for his bestselling biography of Musk -- but Tuesday was the first time he said it under oath.

entries.title: Amazon is already offering new OpenAI products on AWS
entries.description: A day after OpenAI got Microsoft to agree to end exclusive rights, AWS announced a slate of OpenAI model offerings, including a new agent service.

entries.title: Amazon launches an AI-powered audio Q&A experience on product pages
entries.description: Amazon's new "Join the chat" feature lets you ask questions about products and receive AI-powered audio responses.

entries.title: Google expands Pentagon’s access to its AI after Anthropic’s refusal
entries.description: After Anthropic refused to allow the DoD to use its AI for domestic mass surveillance and autonomous weapons, Google has signed a new contract with the department.

entries.title: Lovable launches its vibe-coding app on iOS and Android
entries.description: The app allows developers to vibe code web apps and websites on the go.

entries.title: YouTube is testing an AI-powered search feature that shows guided answers
entries.description: YouTube is rolling out the new AI search feature to Premium subscribers in the U.S. on an opt-in basis.

entries.title: BCI startup Neurable looks to license its ‘mind-reading’ tech for consumer wearables
entries.description: The startup specializes in "non-invasive" "mind-reading" tech — a kind of neural data collection that, its CEO hopes, will have all sorts of consumer applications.

entries.title: Red Hat’s OpenClaw maintainer just made enterprise Claw deployments a lot safer
entries.description: Tank OS puts OpenClaw AI agents into a container that lets it run reliably and more safely, especially for those running fleets of them.

entries.title: Otter’s new feature lets users search across their enterprise tools

entries.title: To buy this Bay Area home, you’ll need Anthropic equity
entries.description: Someone’s offering an unusual deal for a 13-acre property in Mill Valley, just north of San Francisco.

entries.title: Anthropic created a test marketplace for agent-on-agent commerce
entries.description: In a recent experiment, Anthropic created a classified marketplace where AI agents represented both buyers and sellers, striking real deals for real goods and real money.

entries.title: Maine’s governor vetoes data center moratorium
entries.description: L.D. 307 would have imposed the country’s first statewide moratorium on new data centers — lasting, in this case, until November 1, 2027.

entries.title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge community
entries.description: In a letter to the residents of Tumbler Ridge, Canada, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said he is “deeply sorry” that his company failed to alert law enforcement about the suspect in a recent mass shooting.

entries.title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
entries.description: OpenAI has won major concessions from its largest shareholder, Microsoft, that will allow it to sell products on AWS, while Microsoft gets more cash in a revenue-share agreement.

entries.title: DeepMind’s David Silver just raised $1.1B to build an AI that learns without human data
entries.description: Ineffable Intelligence, a British AI lab founded a mere few months ago by former DeepMind researcher David Silver, has raised $1.1 billion in funding at a valuation of $5.1 billion.
